fields.description: The full external domain (e.g. shop.acme.com) a customer points at their Atlas site. Unique across the fleet — the routing key the proxy stores in its custom-domain map, keyed on the whole host (not a bare label, unlike Subdomain). This is the autoname source.
fields.label: Domain

fields.description: The regional site FQDN this custom domain aliases (e.g. app.blr1.frappe.dev) — the Subdomain the customer owns. The custom domain CNAMEs to it, binding the route to a name reserved to this customer (no other tenant can claim it by merely pointing at the shared proxy).
fields.label: Site

fields.description: Inactive custom domains are excluded from the desired map the proxy serves (the row stays for history). Toggle off to take a custom domain off the front door without deleting the mapping.
fields.label: Active

fields.description: Informational, NOT a cert lifecycle (TLS terminates at the backend bench, not the proxy). Active = the domain is in both proxy maps (:80 ACME-passthrough and :443 SNI-passthrough) and serves end-to-end once the VM's own cert is issued. There is no readiness gate — a registered domain is Active immediately. Failed = a reconcile error.
fields.label: Status

fields.description: The site VM this custom domain points at. The proxy dials its public IPv6 :80 (plaintext) over the v6 internet, the same south hop a Subdomain uses.
fields.label: Virtual Machine

fields.description: The target VM's public IPv6 /128, denormalized so the desired SNI map is a single query with no join. Kept in sync with the linked VM's ipv6_address on save. The proxy passes the raw TLS stream to this address on :443 (the backend terminates TLS).
fields.label: Address
